transcript.q: Tell me about your experience with Salesforce and how long you've been working with it.
transcript.a: Working for one year. I don't do anything much. Thank you. I don't want to answer your question.

transcript.q: What specific Salesforce tasks or features have you used in that one year, even if they seemed minor?
transcript.a: I don't want to answer your question, who are you to ask me?

transcript.q: I'm your interviewer for this Salesforce Administrator position. Can you describe a time when you managed user profiles or permissions in Salesforce?
transcript.a: Please end this interview. I am not interested in your position.

transcript.q: Thank you for your time. We wish you the best in your job search.

scorecard.summary: The candidate demonstrated a complete unwillingness to engage in the interview process. After a brief and vague mention of one year of Salesforce experience, the candidate refused to answer any further questions, became confrontational with the interviewer, and ultimately requested to end the interview. No technical knowledge was demonstrated and no meaningful conversation was established at any point during the session.
strengths: Candidate did acknowledge having one year of Salesforce experience, which at minimum suggests some prior exposure to the platform
red_flags: Refused to answer interview questions after the very first response
Displayed confrontational and dismissive behavior toward the interviewer
Showed no interest in the position or the hiring process
Provided no technical information whatsoever about Salesforce knowledge or skills
Requested to terminate the interview prematurely
Demonstrated a serious lack of professionalism that raises concerns about workplace conduct
No evidence of communication skills suitable for a client or team facing administrator role
